regions.text: How did a man know how many sheep he owned? He used stones, putting one in a bag for each sheep.
The number of stones in the bag was the same as the number of his sheep. They were equal in number.
When a sheep died, he took a stone out. When lambs were born, he put a stone in. The stones were a record.
lamb: baby sheep. In U.S.A. meat from sheep is lamb. In Britain, mutton is used for meat of full-grown sheep.
